How to Post a DJ Mix on YouTube (With Tracklist and Album Art)

March 20, 2025•7 min read

Introduction

YouTube is one of the best platforms for sharing DJ mixes. It offers long-form video support, a massive audience, and strong discovery through search. But uploading a DJ mix without a proper tracklist, timestamps, or artwork looks unprofessional — and submitting without the right preparation can result in a copyright claim or block.

This guide walks you through posting a DJ mix on YouTube the right way.

Step 1: Identify Every Track in Your Mix

Before you upload anything, you need a complete tracklist. This is the most time-consuming step — but it's essential for the description, timestamps, and legal disclosure.

If you recorded your set live and didn't track what you played, 45 Mix Trackr can identify every song automatically. Upload your audio file and it returns the title, artist, and timestamp for every recognized track — usually within a few minutes for a one-hour mix.

Step 2: Record or Export Your Mix

Export your mix as a high-quality audio file:

  • Format: WAV or high-bitrate MP3 (320kbps minimum)
  • Length: YouTube supports videos up to 12 hours on verified accounts
  • Normalization: Aim for -1dB peak to avoid clipping on YouTube's audio processing

Step 3: Create the Video

YouTube requires a video file, not just audio. Options:

Static image video:

The simplest approach. Use a single image (your mix artwork, a photo, or a DJ photo) combined with your audio file. Tools:

  • DaVinci Resolve (free) — import audio + image, export as MP4
  • iMovie — drag in both, export
  • Adobe Premiere — professional option

Tracklist visualization:

More engaging. Use a tool like Adobe Express or Canva to create a video that shows the tracklist scrolling as each song plays.

SRT subtitles:

45 Mix Trackr generates an SRT subtitle file with each song's title and artist timed to when they appear in the mix. Import this into DaVinci Resolve or Premiere and the song names will appear as on-screen text automatically throughout the video.

Step 4: Write the Description with Timestamps

A good YouTube description dramatically increases discoverability. Format it like this:

[Mix Title] — [Your DJ Name]

Tracklist:

0:00 Artist - Track Title

3:24 Artist - Track Title

7:11 Artist - Track Title

...

Recorded live at [Venue/Location] on [Date]

Follow me:

Instagram: @yourhandle

Mixcloud: mixcloud.com/yourname

YouTube automatically converts timestamps in the 0:00 format into clickable chapter markers in the video timeline. This is extremely valuable for viewers who want to find specific tracks.

Step 5: Create Album Artwork

A strong thumbnail is critical for YouTube performance. Use:

  • Your DJ name prominently
  • The genre or mood of the mix
  • A high-quality image (your photo, abstract art, or a relevant scene)
  • Minimum 1280×720 pixels, ideally 1920×1080

45 Mix Trackr downloads the album covers for every recognized track in your mix. These can be used as collage elements in your thumbnail artwork.

Step 6: Handle Copyright

DJ mixes on YouTube almost always receive Content ID claims from rights holders. This is different from a copyright strike — a claim means the rights holder can run ads on your video or block it in specific countries.

To minimize issues:

  • Always include a full tracklist in the description — this demonstrates good faith
  • Consider joining a DJ pools or licensing services if you publish regularly
  • Some rights holders allow DJ mixes without blocking — house and electronic music labels tend to be more permissive than major pop labels

Never dispute a legitimate Content ID claim — false disputes escalate to manual reviews and potential channel strikes.

Step 7: Optimize for Search

YouTube is the world's second largest search engine. Optimize your upload:

  • Title: Include genre, year, mix number, and your name ("Deep House Mix 2025 — Vol. 12")
  • Tags: Artist names, genre tags, "DJ mix", "tracklist"
  • Category: Music
  • Description keywords: Include genre, mood, tempo, occasion

Conclusion

A well-prepared YouTube upload — complete tracklist, timestamps, artwork, and a properly formatted description — is the difference between a mix that disappears and one that builds an audience over time. Use 45 Mix Trackr to automate the hardest part (building the tracklist), create compelling artwork with the album covers it downloads, and use the SRT file for automatic on-screen track credits.

Identify your DJ mix instantly

Upload any audio or video mix and get a full tracklist with song titles, artists, and album covers in minutes.
